commentaries.author: A Treatise Against the Heretic Novatian
commentaries.content: For where there are "rivalries and dissensions among you, are ye not carnal, and walk according to man? "

commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: For he called those who had already believed on the Holy Spirit spiritual, and those newly instructed and not yet purified carnal; whom with justice he calls still carnal, as minding equally with the heathen the things of the flesh: "For whereas there is among you envy and strife, are ye not carnal, and walk as men? "

commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: Whereas there is among you envying and strife . . . are ye not carnal? (1.) The word carnal is here applied to one who not only has his natural use of sense and reason, but also to one who follows the motions and dictates of the flesh, that Isaiah , of his animal nature. And, therefore, as S. Thomas rightly remarks, he who follows the motions of lust, or of his fallen nature, is carnal, natural, walking according to Prayer of Manasseh , and destitute of the Spirit of God. (2.) Both here and in Gal. v19, the works of the flesh, i.e, of our corrupt nature, include envying, jealousy, strife, which are spiritual sins, as well as gluttony and lust, which are, strictly speaking, fleshly. Cf. notes to Romans 7:22, and Galatians 5:17. The meaning is: You, O Corinthians, are carnal, i.e, contentious, because you fight like boys foolishly about the dignity of your teachers, and extol and put up for sale, one Paul, another Apollos.

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: "For when envying and strife "he says, "and dissensions are among you, are ye not carnal, and walk as men? "

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Here Paul talks about the particular problem which made the Corinthians carnal. There were other matters, like fornication and uncleanness, which he would deal with later, but first he wants to tackle something which he has clearly been trying to put right for some time. If jealousy makes people carnal, every one of us ought to be crying out because of our sin and covering ourselves in sackcloth and ashes. Who is not tainted with this? I say this of others only because I know how true it is of me.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: At this point he prepares himself to wrestle with those whose part was obedience: for in what went before he has been casting down the rulers of the Church, where he said that wisdom of speech is nothing worth. But here he strikes at those in subjection, in the words
